prompts.text: Set up the challenge before starting: 'We're looking for two things at once — the right colour AND the right shape. Both have to match.' This dual focus is the core learning.
Core question: 'What colour is it? What shape is it?' — ask both, separately, before they choose. The goal is holding two attributes in mind simultaneously.
When they name both correctly ('It's a red circle'), affirm the compound: 'Yes — red AND circle. You checked both!' — reinforces the two-attribute habit.
Real-world extension: 'Can you find a [colour] [shape] anywhere near us?' — moves two-attribute thinking into the environment.
